# ane-tts

> Real-time text-to-speech on Apple Silicon by leveraging the Neural Engine. Makes 5B TTS models (Fish S2 Pro) run at real-time speed on consumer Macs.

## Problem
Fish S2 Pro (5B params, best open-source TTS) runs at 0.65x RTF on Metal GPU. Sub-realtime = unusable for conversation. The ANE (15.8 TOPS) sits idle during inference.

## Approach
Two backends: direct ANE access (maderix/ANE, biggest speedup) and CoreML (stable, for everyone).
If direct inference isn't enough: speculative TTS decoding — small draft model on ANE, big model verifies on GPU.

## Status
Research phase. Decision tree at docs/DECISION_TREE.md. No inference code yet.

## Key Tools
- maderix/ANE (github.com/maderix/ANE) — direct ANE access, 6.2K stars
- ANEMLL (github.com/Anemll/Anemll) — LLMs on ANE
- coremltools — Apple's official CoreML conversion
- mlx-audio — current TTS inference (Metal GPU)

## Models
- Fish S2 Pro 5B BF16 (~10GB) — target model, best quality
- Qwen3-TTS 1.7B BF16 (~4GB) — potential draft model
- Qwen3-TTS 0.6B 4-bit (~1GB) — small draft model for ANE

## Decision Tree Summary
Phase 0: Benchmark ANE capabilities (maderix/ANE on M2 Max)
Phase 1: Try Fish S2 Pro directly on ANE → if fast, ship it
Phase 2: If not, speculative decode (Qwen draft on ANE + Fish verify on GPU)
Phase 3: Polish, benchmark, package, release
Phase 4: Fallback (quantization fix or pure GPU optimization)
Every outcome produces something publishable. No wasted path.
